name: Egg White
notes: Histamine liberator (mast-cell activating), strongest raw but still active when cooked. The yolk is well tolerated, so the white is the part to avoid or swap. Note: UK dietitians (BDA) rate whole eggs as freely tolerated, so reactions are individual.

name: Egg Yolk
notes: Well tolerated and free of histamine concerns — the safe part of the egg, unlike the white.

name: Whole Egg
notes: The yolk is fine but the white is a histamine liberator (especially raw), so whole egg is best limited. BDA considers eggs freely tolerated, so sensitivity varies.

name: Quail Egg
notes: Well tolerated, no histamine concerns.

name: Blue Cheese
notes: Mould-ripened and aged — among the highest-histamine cheeses, also rich in tyramine and cadaverine. Avoid; swap for a fresh cheese like ricotta or mozzarella.

name: Cultured Butter
notes: Soured with bacteria, so carries small amounts of histamine; usually still tolerated. Sweet-cream butter is the safer choice.

name: Sweet Cream Butter
notes: Ordinary unfermented butter, well tolerated.

name: Butterkäse
notes: Young, mild cheese, well tolerated.

name: Buttermilk
notes: Lactic-acid fermented, so slightly histamine-forming; small amounts are often tolerated.

name: Cheddar
notes: Aged hard cheese — high in histamine and other amines, with reported levels spanning 0–2000 mg/kg, and one of the cheeses named in histamine-poisoning outbreaks. Avoid; swap for a fresh cheese.

name: Raw Milk Cheese
notes: Made from unpasteurised milk, so a higher bacterial load means more histamine and amines; riskier than pasteurised cheese.

name: Hard Cheese
notes: All well-matured cheeses are strongly histamine-rich and also high in tyramine, building up the longer they age — a classic trigger. Choose fresh, unripened cheese instead.

name: Cream Cheese
notes: Very young, unripened cheese — well tolerated if additive-free.

name: Sweet Cream
notes: Plain unfermented cream is well tolerated; check for thickeners and stabilisers, which are often the real problem.

name: Curd Cheese
notes: Fresh, unripened cheese, well tolerated.

name: Sheep's Milk
notes: Plain sheep's milk is well tolerated.

name: Farmer's Cheese
notes: A fresh, unripened cheese, well tolerated.

name: Feta
notes: Brined cheese with some histamine and amines; small amounts are often tolerated.

name: Fontina
notes: Semi-aged cheese, histamine-rich — best avoided.

name: Geheimratskäse
notes: Mild young cheese, well tolerated.

name: Goat's Milk
notes: Plain goat's milk is well tolerated.

name: Young Gouda
notes: Only lightly aged — tolerated in small amounts, unlike aged Gouda.

name: Aged Gouda
notes: Long-matured, so histamine builds up, unlike young Gouda; Gouda is among the cheeses linked to histamine-poisoning outbreaks. Best avoided.

name: Kefir
notes: Fermented milk drink carrying histamine and amines; small amounts are sometimes tolerated.

name: Mascarpone
notes: Fresh cream cheese, well tolerated.

name: Lactose-Free Milk
notes: Plain milk with lactose removed; may carry a little histamine and is sometimes slightly less tolerated than regular milk.

name: Pasteurised Milk
notes: Fresh pasteurised or UHT milk is low in histamine and well tolerated (may bother an already-irritated gut).

name: Milk Powder
notes: Dried milk; tolerance varies, sometimes slightly worse than fresh.

name: Mozzarella
notes: Fresh, unripened cheese, well tolerated — a good swap for aged cheese.

name: Processed Cheese
notes: Made from aged cheese plus additives — histamine-rich, best avoided.

name: Quark
notes: Fresh, unripened dairy, well tolerated.

name: Raclette
notes: Melting cheese, matured and histamine-rich — avoid.

name: Raw Milk
notes: Highly perishable from its bacterial load; histamine forms quickly as it sits, so use only very fresh.

name: Ready-Made Cheese Preparation
notes: Mixed cheese product with added ingredients; tolerance depends on contents and freshness — treat as risky.

name: Ricotta
notes: Fresh whey cheese, well tolerated — a good swap for aged cheese.

name: Roquefort
notes: Blue mould cheese — among the highest in histamine, tyramine and cadaverine. Avoid.

name: Sour Cream
notes: Lactic-acid fermented, so slightly histamine-containing; small amounts are often tolerated.

name: Whey
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Yoghurt
notes: Fermented, so histamine varies by product and culture; small amounts are often tolerated. BDA notes levels depend on the bacterial strain.

name: Beef
notes: Fresh beef is well tolerated, but meat forms histamine fast as it ages — use very fresh or freshly frozen.

name: Chicken
notes: Fresh chicken is well tolerated; freshness is key as poultry spoils quickly.

name: Dried Meat
notes: Any dried or aged meat is histamine-rich with other amines — a classic trigger. Avoid; use fresh meat.

name: Dry-Cured Ham
notes: Cured and aged (prosciutto, serrano) — a ripened meat high in histamine and tyramine. Avoid; swap for fresh cooked meat.

name: Duck
notes: Fresh duck is well tolerated; depends on freshness.

name: Entrails
notes: Offal spoils very fast and acts as a histamine liberator — best avoided.

name: Game
notes: Usually hung or matured, so histamine builds up; fresh game is better tolerated.

name: Venison
notes: Typically matured meat, so somewhat histamine-rich; fresh is better tolerated.

name: Minced Meat
notes: Large surface area spoils fast, so open or pre-packed mince is histamine-rich. Only fresh, just-ground meat eaten immediately is safe.

name: Ostrich
notes: Fresh ostrich is well tolerated; depends on freshness.

name: Pork
notes: Fresh pork is mostly tolerated but very perishable and can act as a mild liberator (itching). BDA lists pork, bacon and ham as higher-risk.

name: Poultry
notes: Fresh poultry is well tolerated; freshness matters as it spoils quickly.

name: Quail
notes: Fresh quail is well tolerated; depends on freshness.

name: Salami
notes: Dry-fermented sausage — consistently high in histamine and among the highest of any fermented food for tyramine, a classic trigger. Avoid.

name: Sausage
notes: Most sausages are histamine-rich, and fermented types are also high in tyramine; only a few fresh ones are exceptions. Best avoided.

name: Smoked Fish
notes: Smoked and stored — high in histamine. Avoid; choose fresh or freshly frozen fish.

name: Smoked Meat
notes: Smoked and aged — high in histamine. Avoid.

name: Tongue
notes: Tolerated if fresh and unprocessed; avoid smoked or ready-to-eat versions.

name: Turkey
notes: Fresh turkey is well tolerated; freshness matters.

name: Veal
notes: Fresh veal is well tolerated; perishable, so use fresh.

name: Anchovies
notes: Small oily fish, salted and aged — when dried they top the rankings for histamine among all foods, and they also carry other amines. Avoid.

name: Fresh Fish
notes: Freshly caught or frozen fish is tolerated, but fish forms histamine extremely fast — freshness is everything. Oily scombroid species (tuna, mackerel) are riskiest.

name: Stored Fish
notes: Fish left on ice or chilled slowly accumulates histamine — the leading cause of histamine food poisoning (scombroid poisoning), and the histamine isn't removed by later cooking. Only very fresh or freshly frozen fish is safe.

name: Trout
notes: Freshwater trout is tolerated only when very fresh; it forms histamine rapidly.

name: Tuna
notes: Scombroid fish high in free histidine — builds histamine fast if not chilled quickly, and once it has formed, cooking or canning will not remove it. Avoid.

name: Bivalves
notes: Mussels, oysters and similar spoil fast and act as histamine liberators. Avoid.

name: Crab
notes: Spoils fast and acts as a histamine liberator. Avoid.

name: Crawfish
notes: Spoils fast and acts as a histamine liberator. Avoid.

name: Langouste
notes: Spiny lobster — perishable and a histamine liberator. Avoid.

name: Lobster
notes: Perishable and a histamine liberator. Avoid.

name: Prawn
notes: Prawns and shrimp spoil fast and act as histamine liberators. Avoid.

name: Shellfish
notes: Perishable and histamine-liberating — a common trigger. Avoid.

name: Seafood
notes: Seafood generally spoils fast and liberates histamine. Avoid; choose fresh white fish instead.

name: Lard
notes: Pure animal fat, well tolerated.

name: Amaranth
notes: Pseudo-grain, well tolerated (may loosen stools in some).

name: Baked Goods
notes: Usually fine; any trouble comes from malt, long yeast or sourdough fermentation, or certain wheat proteins rather than histamine.

name: Barley
notes: Generally tolerated; watch for added malt.

name: Malt
notes: From long fermentation — a common hidden trigger in breads and cereals. Best avoided.

name: Bread
notes: Usually tolerated; issues come from malt, long fermentation, or wheat proteins, not histamine itself.

name: Buckwheat
notes: Reported as poorly tolerated unless thoroughly peeled; use with caution.

name: Chestnut
notes: Sweet chestnut, well tolerated.

name: Cornflakes
notes: Well tolerated if free of malt or folic-acid additives.

name: Hemp Seeds
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Khorasan Wheat
notes: Old wheat variety (Kamut), usually well tolerated; modern high-ATI wheats are worse.

name: Maltodextrin
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Millet
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Oats
notes: Well tolerated (may cause wind in some).

name: Sago
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Potato
notes: Well tolerated; store dark and cut away any green spots.

name: Quinoa
notes: Generally well tolerated.

name: Rice
notes: Well tolerated; refrigerate cooked rice promptly and use within a day.

name: Rice Cakes
notes: Well tolerated; slightly less so than freshly cooked rice.

name: Rice Crispies
notes: Well tolerated; watch for malt or folic acid.

name: Rice Noodles
notes: Well tolerated; slightly less so than freshly cooked rice.

name: Rye
notes: Barely tolerated for some; usually fine in moderation.

name: Spelt
notes: Well tolerated, especially old varieties; modern high-ATI strains less so.

name: Sunflower Seeds
notes: Act as a histamine liberator — best avoided despite being a plant food.

name: Sweet Corn
notes: Well tolerated; can be hard to digest.

name: Sweet Potato
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Wheat
notes: Usually tolerated; any trouble is typically digestive (wind) rather than histamine.

name: Wheat Germ
notes: Rich in other amines (putrescine, spermidine) and acts as a liberator — best avoided.

name: Wild Rice
notes: Well tolerated (not botanically rice).

name: Yam
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Almond
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts; can occasionally cause issues. BDA rates almonds as freely tolerated.

name: Brazil Nut
notes: Well tolerated; 1–2 a day is a good selenium source.

name: Cashew
notes: Carries some amines and can liberate histamine; small amounts are often tolerated.

name: Tiger Nut
notes: Actually a tuber, not a nut — well tolerated raw.

name: Roasted Tiger Nut
notes: Roasting makes this tuber less tolerated than raw; best limited.

name: Hazelnut
notes: Mild histamine liberator; small amounts are usually tolerated. BDA rates hazelnuts as freely tolerated.

name: Macadamia
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Nuts
notes: Mixed or unspecified nuts vary widely; some (walnut, cashew) are triggers — check the specific nut. BDA considers most nuts freely tolerated.

name: Peanut
notes: SIGHI rates peanuts as incompatible, though BDA lists them as freely tolerated — tolerance is individual.

name: Pine Nuts
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts; species vary.

name: Pistachio
notes: SIGHI rates pistachios as tolerated; usually fine in small amounts.

name: Walnut
notes: Rich in other amines and a histamine liberator — a common trigger. Use sparingly.

name: Black Caraway Oil
notes: Nigella / black-seed oil, well tolerated and noted as anti-allergic.

name: Canola Oil
notes: Rapeseed oil, well tolerated.

name: Coconut Oil
notes: Well tolerated and recommended.

name: Margarine
notes: Tolerated; check for incompatible additives.

name: Olive Oil
notes: Well tolerated (may bother those with salicylate intolerance).

name: Palm Kernel Oil
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Palm Oil
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Pumpkin Seed Oil
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Safflower Oil
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Sunflower Oil
notes: Fine in a single dose; inflammatory if used heavily long-term.

name: Walnut Oil
notes: Reported as incompatible — best avoided; use olive or coconut oil instead.

name: Artichoke
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Asparagus
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Aubergine
notes: One of the few plant foods genuinely high in histamine, and very variable. Avoid; boiling lowers the histamine.

name: Avocado
notes: Notably high in histamine and a liberator — a common trigger despite being a 'healthy fat'.

name: Bamboo Shoots
notes: Tolerance unknown in SIGHI, however BDA 2025 lists them as fine. Use them, but observe your reaction to bamboo shoots.

name: Beans
notes: Pulses act as histamine liberators; most varieties are best avoided, though some are tolerated.

name: Broad Bean
notes: Fava beans are reported as incompatible — best avoided.

name: Beetroot
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Hot Bell Pepper
notes: Hot peppers are irritating and reported as incompatible; use sweet pepper instead.

name: Sweet Bell Pepper
notes: Well tolerated — a good swap for tomato in sauces.

name: Bok Choy
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Broccoli
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Brussels Sprouts
notes: Mild liberator; small amounts are usually tolerated.

name: Cabbage
notes: Well tolerated raw or cooked (fermented cabbage is not).

name: Carrot
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cauliflower
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Celery
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Napa Cabbage
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Chard
notes: Usually tolerated in moderate amounts.

name: Chayote
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Chickpeas
notes: Reported as incompatible; their polyamine content may explain it. Best limited.

name: Chicory
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Chili Pepper
notes: Hot and irritating to the gut — reported as incompatible.

name: Lamb's Lettuce
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Courgette
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cucumber
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Garden Cress
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Endive
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Fennel
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Garlic
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts, especially cooked.

name: Kohlrabi
notes: Usually tolerated.

name: Gourds
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Green Beans
notes: Often tolerated in small amounts.

name: Green Peas
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts (can be higher in putrescine).

name: Split Peas
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts.

name: Horseradish
notes: Acts as a mild liberator; small amounts are usually tolerated.

name: Leek
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts.

name: Lentils
notes: Reported as incompatible; a higher polyamine content may explain it. Best limited.

name: Iceberg Lettuce
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Lettuce
notes: Well tolerated (rating is for the plain leaf, no dressing).

name: Mung Bean Sprouts
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Olives
notes: Usually fermented and brined, so reported as incompatible; check for added ingredients.

name: Onion
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts; a mild liberator and harder to tolerate in quantity.

name: Parsnip
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Wall Rocket
notes: Wild rocket acts as a liberator — best avoided.

name: Pickled Cabbage
notes: Fermented — high in histamine. Avoid.

name: Pickled Cucumber
notes: Pickled and fermented, so histamine-rich. Avoid; use fresh cucumber.

name: Pickled Vegetables
notes: Pickling and fermentation build histamine, and fermented vegetables are a high-histamine food category. Avoid; use fresh.

name: Pumpkin
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Radish
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Red Cabbage
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Sauerkraut
notes: Fermented cabbage — fermented vegetables rank among the highest-histamine food categories, and it is unanimously excluded across low-histamine diets. Avoid.

name: Savoy Cabbage
notes: Usually tolerated.

name: Snow Peas
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts.

name: Soy
notes: Soybeans and soy flour are reported as incompatible; a high polyamine content may explain it. Best avoided.

name: Spinach
notes: One of the few plant foods genuinely high in histamine, and very variable — rises further with storage. Boiling lowers it.

name: Stinging Nettle
notes: Reported high in histamine — best avoided.

name: Tomato
notes: High in histamine and a liberator — a classic trigger, even more concentrated as puree or ketchup. Swap with roasted red pepper or a carrot-based sauce.

name: Turnip
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: White Onion
notes: Mild, sweet onion variety — well tolerated, unlike common onion in quantity.

name: Basil
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Wild Garlic
notes: Mild liberator; small amounts are well tolerated.

name: Blue Fenugreek
notes: A clover / trigonella herb reported as incompatible — best avoided.

name: Chives
notes: Usually tolerated; harder to tolerate in large amounts.

name: Clover
notes: Trigonella and trifolium herbs (e.g. fenugreek) are reported as incompatible — best avoided.

name: Dill
notes: Small amounts are usually fine (high in salicylates).

name: Fenugreek
notes: Reported as incompatible — best avoided.

name: Mint
notes: Well tolerated (may bother salicylate-sensitive people).

name: Oregano
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Parsley
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Rosemary
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Sage
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Savory
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Acerola
notes: Well tolerated; rich in vitamin C.

name: Apple
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Apricot
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Aronia
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Asimina Triloba
notes: Pawpaw — no reliable rating, tolerance unknown.

name: Banana
notes: Low in histamine but high in putrescine, which can block histamine breakdown — a common trigger; greener fruit is better tolerated.

name: Prickly Pear
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Blackberry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Blackcurrant
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Blueberry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Boysenberry
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Starfruit
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cherry
notes: Well tolerated (somewhat debated).

name: Citrus Fruits
notes: Low in histamine but high in putrescine and acting as liberators — frequently trigger symptoms despite little actual histamine.

name: Cocoa Butter
notes: Mostly well tolerated (the fat, not the cocoa solids).

name: Cocoa
notes: Cocoa and chocolate carry amines (tyramine, phenylethylamine) and liberate histamine — a common trigger.

name: Coconut
notes: Well tolerated; a good selenium source.

name: Sea Buckthorn
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cowberry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cranberry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Dates
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Rose Hip
notes: Mild liberator; usually tolerated.

name: Dragon Fruit
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Figs
notes: Usually tolerated; may be slightly laxative.

name: Goji Berry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Gooseberry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Grapefruit
notes: Citrus — high in putrescine and a liberator; a frequent trigger despite low histamine.

name: Grapes
notes: Well tolerated as fresh fruit (wine is not).

name: Guava
notes: Reported as incompatible — best limited.

name: Jostaberry
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Kaki
notes: Persimmon / sharon fruit, well tolerated.

name: Kiwi
notes: Acts as a histamine liberator; SIGHI flags it while milder lists rate it lower-risk — usually fine in small amounts.

name: Lemon
notes: Citrus — putrescine-rich and a liberator; a frequent trigger despite low histamine.

name: Lemon Zest
notes: Concentrated citrus peel — a liberator; best avoided.

name: Lime
notes: Citrus, more poorly tolerated than lemon — a putrescine-rich liberator. Avoid.

name: Loganberry
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Lychee
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Mandarin
notes: Citrus — very high in putrescine; a common trigger.

name: Mango
notes: Usually well tolerated (somewhat debated).

name: Melon
notes: Well tolerated (except watermelon, which is debated).

name: Morello Cherry
notes: Sour cherry, well tolerated.

name: Mulberry
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Nashi Pear
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Nectarine
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Orange
notes: Citrus — a putrescine-rich liberator and poorly tolerated; a frequent trigger.

name: Orange Zest
notes: Concentrated citrus peel — a liberator; best avoided.

name: Papaya
notes: Carries amines and acts as a liberator — best avoided.

name: Passion Fruit
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Peach
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Pear
notes: Usually tolerated; carries a little of the other amines.

name: Pineapple
notes: Acts as a liberator and carries amines — a common trigger.

name: Plum
notes: Mild liberator; usually tolerated in moderation.

name: Pomegranate
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Quince
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Raisins
notes: Well tolerated if unsulphured and preservative-free; high in salicylates.

name: Raspberry
notes: Reported as incompatible — best limited.

name: Redcurrant
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Rhubarb
notes: Usually tolerated (contains oxalic acid; somewhat debated).

name: Strawberry
notes: A classic histamine 'liberator' — frequently triggers symptoms though it contains little actual histamine.

name: Tamarillo
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Watermelon
notes: Usually tolerated; a suspected mild liberator.

name: Chia Seeds
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Psyllium Husk
notes: Well tolerated; useful fibre.

name: Pumpkin Seeds
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Sesame
notes: Usually tolerated in small amounts (may loosen stools).

name: Algae
notes: Seaweeds (nori, kombu, wakame, kelp) act as liberators and are extremely iodine-rich — best avoided.

name: Cep
notes: Porcini — reported as incompatible like most mushrooms.

name: Morel
notes: Reported as incompatible.

name: Mushroom
notes: Mushrooms are generally reported as incompatible and are high in polyamines. Best limited.

name: Button Mushroom
notes: Carries some amines; small amounts are usually tolerated — better than aged mushrooms.

name: Yeast
notes: Generally tolerated fresh; long-fermented doughs can be a problem, and it is high in glutamate.

name: Agave Syrup
notes: Well tolerated (high in fructose).

name: Artificial Sweeteners
notes: Generally tolerated (sucralose in particular).

name: Xylitol
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Caramel
notes: Plain browned sugar, well tolerated.

name: Dextrose
notes: Pure glucose, well tolerated and fructose-free.

name: Sorbitol
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Malt Extract
notes: From fermentation — reported as incompatible; best avoided.

name: Fructose
notes: Well tolerated (excess can upset digestion).

name: Honey
notes: Generally tolerated (naturally contains benzoic acid; somewhat debated).

name: Invert Sugar
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Lactose
notes: Milk sugar, well tolerated.

name: Liquorice Root
notes: Reported as incompatible — best avoided.

name: Maltose
notes: Pure malt sugar, well tolerated.

name: Maple Syrup
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Palm Sugar
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Stevia
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Sugar
notes: Well tolerated; use sparingly.

name: Anise
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Bay Leaf
notes: Small amounts are well tolerated; less is known about larger quantities.

name: Black Caraway
notes: Nigella / black seed, well tolerated and noted as anti-allergic.

name: Bouillon
notes: Stock cubes usually contain glutamate or yeast extract — reported as incompatible. Use fresh homemade stock.

name: Caraway
notes: Well tolerated (don't confuse with cumin, which is a trigger).

name: Cardamom
notes: Generally well tolerated; use modestly.

name: Cilantro
notes: Fresh coriander leaf, tolerated in small amounts.

name: Cinnamon
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cloves
notes: Small amounts are well tolerated.

name: Coriander
notes: Tolerated in small amounts.

name: Cumin
notes: Acts as a liberator — reported as incompatible. Swap with caraway.

name: Curry
notes: Spice blend reported as incompatible (often contains chilli and more). Best avoided.

name: White Vinegar
notes: Distilled / spirit vinegar is low in histamine and usually tolerated in small amounts, unlike wine vinegars. BDA also rates vinegars as low.

name: Ginger
notes: Small amounts are well tolerated.

name: Juniper Berries
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Meat Extract
notes: Concentrated, carrying amines and glutamate — reported as incompatible. Avoid.

name: Mustard Seeds
notes: Act as a liberator — best avoided.

name: Nutmeg
notes: Small amounts are well tolerated.

name: Hot Paprika
notes: Irritating to the gut — reported as incompatible; use sweet paprika.

name: Sweet Paprika
notes: Well tolerated — a good swap for hot paprika.

name: Black Pepper
notes: Reported as incompatible; small amounts are tolerated by some.

name: White Pepper
notes: Reported as incompatible; small amounts are tolerated by some.

name: Poppy Seeds
notes: Small amounts are well tolerated.

name: Red Wine Vinegar
notes: Made from wine — high in histamine. Avoid; use distilled white vinegar.

name: Hydrolysed Protein Seasoning
notes: Contains glutamate, histamine and other amines — reported as incompatible. Avoid.

name: Soy Sauce
notes: Fermented — high in histamine and other amines. Avoid; try a tamarind-based seasoning instead.

name: Star Anise
notes: No reliable rating — tolerance unknown.

name: Thyme
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Turmeric
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Vanilla Extract
notes: Tolerated in small amounts (from fermentation; alcoholic).

name: Vanilla
notes: Tolerated in small amounts (fermentation; possible sulphite traces).

name: Apple Cider Vinegar
notes: Carries a little histamine; check for additives — usually tolerated in small amounts.

name: Balsamic Vinegar
notes: Aged wine vinegar — high in histamine. Avoid; use distilled white vinegar.

name: White Wine Vinegar
notes: Made from wine — high in histamine. Avoid; use distilled white vinegar.

name: Yeast Extract
notes: Concentrated yeast (Marmite-type) — reported to contain histamine and tyramine, converts to glutamate, and acts as a liberator. Avoid.

name: Still Mineral Water
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Tap Water
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Ethanol
notes: Pure alcohol both releases histamine and blocks the enzyme that clears it — poorly tolerated.

name: Alcoholic Beverages
notes: Alcohol carries histamine and amines, liberates histamine, and blocks DAO — a strong, well-known trigger overall.

name: Beer
notes: Fermented and alcoholic — carries histamine and is a notable source of tyramine, while the alcohol blocks DAO; unanimously excluded across low-histamine diets. Avoid.

name: Brandy
notes: Distilled from wine — histamine-rich and DAO-blocking. Avoid.

name: Champagne
notes: Sparkling wine — histamine-rich, a liberator and a DAO-blocker. Avoid.

name: Clear Liquor
notes: Clear spirits liberate histamine and block DAO, though lower in histamine than dark or cloudy ones. BDA allows spirits in moderation.

name: Cloudy Liquor
notes: Cloudy spirits carry histamine and amines and block DAO. Avoid.

name: Rum
notes: Histamine-rich spirit that also blocks DAO. Avoid.

name: Wine
notes: Fermented and alcoholic — histamine-rich, a liberator and a DAO-blocker; unanimously excluded across low-histamine diets. Avoid.

name: Histamine-Free Wine
notes: Specially made low-histamine wine; still contains alcohol and sulphite. Better tolerated, especially once cooked off in food.

name: Red Wine
notes: The highest-histamine wine, also a liberator and DAO-blocker. Avoid.

name: White Wine
notes: Histamine-rich, liberating and DAO-blocking. Avoid.

name: Chamomile Tea
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Green Tea
notes: Mildly blocks DAO; usually tolerated in moderation.

name: Herbal Tea
notes: Usually tolerated; complex blends with many herbs can occasionally cause issues.

name: Lime Blossom Tea
notes: Well tolerated; calming.

name: Mate Tea
notes: Mildly blocks DAO; tolerated in moderation.

name: Peppermint Tea
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Rooibos Tea
notes: Well tolerated; check blends for added citrus peel.

name: Sage Tea
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Nettle Tea
notes: Carries a little histamine; usually tolerated in moderation.

name: Black Tea
notes: High in histamine and blocks DAO — best avoided; choose herbal or rooibos.

name: Verbena Tea
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Cranberry Juice
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Orange Juice
notes: Citrus juice acts as a liberator and is putrescine-rich — a common trigger.

name: Tomato Juice
notes: Concentrated tomato — a liberator and common trigger. Avoid.

name: Cola
notes: Usually tolerated in moderation (caffeine can stimulate).

name: Coffee
notes: Usually tolerated; caffeine can stimulate the gut and mast cells. BDA lists coffee as higher-risk (pods lowest).

name: Energy Drink
notes: Contains theobromine, which blocks DAO — best avoided.

name: Espresso
notes: Usually tolerated; gentler than regular coffee but still caffeinated.

name: Oat Milk
notes: Often slightly histamine-containing from enzymatic processing; usually tolerated.

name: Rice Milk
notes: Often slightly histamine-containing from processing; usually tolerated.

name: Soy Milk
notes: Made from soy — reported as incompatible. Try oat or rice milk.

name: Chocolate Drink
notes: Cocoa-based — carries amines and liberates histamine. Avoid.

name: Elderflower Cordial
notes: Well tolerated.

name: Lemonade
notes: Tolerance depends on ingredients; usually fine.

name: Soda
notes: Tolerance depends on ingredients; usually fine in moderation.

name: Iodized Salt
notes: Added iodine acts as a liberator — choose plain non-iodised salt.

name: Liquorice
notes: Acts as a liberator — best avoided.

name: Marzipan
notes: Almond paste, tolerated in small amounts if additive-free.

name: Dark Chocolate
notes: Carries tyramine and phenylethylamine — a common trigger. White chocolate is milder.

name: White Chocolate
notes: Contains no cocoa solids, so milder — usually tolerated in small amounts.

name: Prepared Mustard
notes: Made with mustard seed and vinegar — histamine-rich and liberating. Avoid.

name: Tofu
notes: Reported as incompatible; fermented soy products are worse. Best limited.

name: Pasta
notes: Refrigerate leftover cooked pasta promptly and use within a day.
